name: Refried Beans
recipeInstructions:
1. Pick over beans to remove rocks and bad beans.
2. Wash the beans and place them in a large pot with sugar.
3. Add enough cold water to cover, then heat to boiling.
4. Cover and simmer for 3 hours or until beans are soft, adding additional water as needed during cooking.
5. Stir in 1/2 cup bacon drippings, salt, and garlic. Cook for at least 1 hour more, stirring occasionally.
6. If desired, cook for more than 4 hours or overnight, ensuring there is enough water.
7. Heat 1/4 cup bacon drippings or lard in a large skillet and add the beans.
8. Stir frequently until beans are reheated.
9. Mash with a fork or potato masher.
10. Brown beans until dry and slightly crusty, stirring frequently.
11. Add salt to taste if needed.
Note: Begin with water at least twice as deep as beans. When beans have finished swelling, add liquid as needed to keep the level above the top of the beans. Bacon drippings or lard provide the best flavor, but vegetable oil may be substituted for those concerned with cholesterol levels. If desired, grated cheese, chopped chilis, diced onion, or crumbled bacon can be added during frying. If beans become too thick, thin with a little milk.
